home.title: Traditional Musical Instruments of Nepal
home.subtitle: Interactive Digital Learning Platform for Cultural Preservation and Education
home.mockSarangiDesc: The Sarangi is a traditional bowed string instrument carved from a single piece of wood. It produces haunting, soulful melodies.
home.mockMadalDesc: A cylindrical drum with two heads. Essential in Nepali folk music and dance performances.
home.mockBansuriDesc: A flute made from bamboo, revered across the Himalayan regions for its ethereal sound.
home.whySubtitle: Preserving Nepal's rich musical heritage through digital innovation
home.culturalPreservationDesc: Document and safeguard traditional instruments and playing techniques for future generations through digital archiving.
home.digitalLearningDesc: Make traditional music education accessible to everyone through interactive online resources and expert guidance.
home.threeDDesc: Explore instruments in detail with interactive 3D models, understanding construction and playing techniques.
home.expertKnowledgeDesc: Learn directly from master musicians and scholars who have dedicated their lives to these traditions.
home.selectInstrumentDesc: Browse our collection of traditional Nepali instruments organized by category, region, or type.
home.exploreThreeDDesc: Interact with detailed 3D models and listen to authentic recordings of each instrument.
home.learnFromExpertsDesc: Watch videos and read insights from master musicians and cultural scholars.
home.ctaDesc: Join us in preserving and celebrating Nepal's musical heritage. Start your journey into traditional Nepali music today.
home.discoverInstruments: Discover traditional Nepali instruments from different regions and categories

instruments.title: Instruments
instruments.subtitle: Explore Nepal's rich collection of traditional instruments

experts.title: Experts
experts.subtitle: Learn from Master Musicians
experts.intro: Our experts are master musicians, ethnomusicologists, and cultural preservationists who have dedicated their lives to traditional Nepali music. Each brings unique knowledge and years of experience in their respective fields.
experts.shareExpertiseDesc: Are you a master musician, researcher, or cultural expert? Be a part of our initiative to share your knowledge and help preserve Nepal's musical heritage.
experts.shareKnowledgeDesc: Contribute to cultural preservation through teaching and documentation
experts.reachStudentsDesc: Connect with learners from around the world interested in Nepali music
experts.preserveTraditionsDesc: Help ensure traditional techniques and knowledge are passed to future generations

learn.title: Learn
learn.subtitle: Comprehensive educational resources on traditional Nepali musical instruments
learn.chooseTutorial: Choose a tutorial to start playing.

about.title: About This Project
about.subtitle: Preserving Nepal's Musical Heritage Through Digital Innovation
about.introText: Traditional Nepali musical instruments carry centuries of cultural wisdom, artistic expression, and community identity. As modernization accelerates, many of these instruments and the stories behind them risk fading into silence
about.teamIntro: This platform was developed by a dedicated team of engineering students committed to preserving Nepal's cultural heritage through technology.
about.developerEducation: Bachelor of Engineering (Electronics, Communication & Information Engineering)
about.developerCollege: Advanced College of Engineering and Management
about.joinMissionDesc: Help us preserve and promote Nepal's musical heritage. Whether you're a musician, researcher, donor, or enthusiast, there are many ways to contribute.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Get in touch with us for questions, collaborations, or feedback

contact.formIntro: Have questions about the project, interested in contributing, or want to learn more? Fill out the form below and we'll get back to you as soon as possible.

footer.aboutPlatformDesc: Dedicated to preserving and promoting the rich heritage of traditional Nepali musical instruments through digital innovation and education.
footer.supportedBy: Supported by: Nepal Government, Ministry of Education, Science and Technology (MoEST)

error.pageNotFoundDesc: Sorry, the page you're looking for doesn't exist.

instrumentDetail.noTutorials: No tutorial videos are available for this instrument yet.
instrumentDetail.sourcesAcknowledgmentsText: This content was developed through expert consultation, field research, institutional visits, and references from published materials on traditional Nepali musical instruments.

tuner.pageSubtitle: Select an instrument and tuning standard, then tune with live feedback.
tuner.noStandardConfigured: No tuning standard configured for this instrument.
tuner.micBlocked: Microphone access was blocked. Please allow microphone access in your browser.
tuner.noMic: No microphone found. Please connect a microphone and try again.
tuner.allowMic: Please allow microphone access in your browser settings to use the tuner.
tuner.step2: Hold the microphone close to your instrument
tuner.step3: Adjust the instrument until it matches the target note
tuner.step4: The indicator shows if you're sharp or flat
tuner.step5: Green dot means your note is in tune

viewer3d.controlsHelp: Click and drag to rotate • Scroll to zoom • Right-click to pan
viewer3d.validFormat: Please ensure the file is a valid .glb or .gltf format
viewer3d.gltfNote: .gltf files require a .bin buffer file in the same directory
